meaning: n.尘垢,污点,煤尘vt.使污秽,污浊
examples: Huge chimneys belched forth smoke and grime.
Kelly got the grime off his hands before rejoining her in the kitchen.
Its true hues may become obscured by grime and even buildups of salt.
Engineers in Germany are busily using nanotechnology to protect surfaces from dirt and grime.
The outermost part of this layer prevents other particles, like dirt and grime, from binding to it.
